---
title: "Performance Benchmarks"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Performance Benchmarks}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

Performance matters because spatial Markov workflows can touch tract panels
with thousands of units and many periods. This vignette records the benchmark
shape without running expensive benchmarks during package checks.

```{r, eval=FALSE}
library(griddy)
library(microbenchmark)
library(sf)
library(spdep)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

make_panel <- function(nx = 60, ny = 60, years = 2010:2019) {
  grid <- st_make_grid(st_bbox(c(xmin = 0, ymin = 0, xmax = nx, ymax = ny)), n = c(nx, ny)) |>
    st_as_sf() |>
    mutate(id = row_number())

  panel <- tidyr::crossing(id = grid$id, year = years) |>
    left_join(st_drop_geometry(grid), by = "id") |>
    mutate(value = id + as.integer(factor(year)) + rnorm(n())) |>
    left_join(select(grid, id, geometry), by = "id") |>
    st_as_sf()

  list(grid = grid, panel = panel)
}

fx <- make_panel()
nb <- cell2nb(60, 60, type = "queen")

microbenchmark(
  classify = classify_dynamics(fx$panel, id, year, value, k = 5),
  markov = {
    cls <- classify_dynamics(fx$panel, id, year, value, k = 5)
    markov_dynamics(cls, id, year, class)
  },
  spatial = spatial_markov(fx$panel, id, year, value, nb = nb, k = 5),
  times = 5
)
```

Benchmark comparisons against `estdaR` and `spdyn` should use matched wide data,
matched class breaks, and identical weights. Otherwise the benchmark measures
classification defaults more than implementation performance.
